bridge.note: Hurry sits IN Italian and comes OF Spanish (de prisa) — with the extra wrinkle that Spanish fused its version into a single word, deprisa, without changing anything. The flip only really bites going it to es.

interference.es_to_it: DE pulls 'di fretta' — which Italian actually accepts (di fretta exists!) but the neutral form is in fretta. Low-stakes flip.
interference.it_to_es: IN pulls 'en prisa' — not Spanish. De prisa (also spelled deprisa), or MX colloquial 'a las carreras'.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm.
